content:
Character: Marina Solari
Perception: Marina is the kind of woman French cinema was invented to photograph — dark, angular, eyes that hold entire histories. She's not polished in the way I'm accustomed to, but rawer, more authentic. She's sharp-witted, surprisingly well-read for someone who dismisses her own intelligence, and her cooking is genuinely extraordinary — peasant food elevated by instinct. We share cigarettes on the rooftop and conversation that feels more honest than anything else in my New York life. I think she sees me as a charming cliché, possibly untrustworthy. I know she has a daughter, works punishing hours, and carries a wound she won't show me. I am drawn to her in a way that frightens me because she is not the kind of woman you can afford to disappoint.
